methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
Terra Industries is a Series A–stage defense and security company founded by Nathan Nwachuku and Maxwell Maduka that has raised $34M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 10 private companies and 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) operating in the defense and security sector at the same stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a valuation proxy, applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage advancement, implying a $68M valuation. Peer-median methodology surveys four same-sector, same-stage companies—Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), Ares Industries ($150M), and NODA AI ($125M)—yielding a median valuation of $225M and a range of $125M to $500M. The peer-median approach carries more weight given the company's position within an active, comparable cohort.

Triangulation across both methods yields a median valuation of $146M, with a low of $58M and a high of $259M. The wide range reflects material uncertainty regarding Terra's product maturity, customer traction, and unit economics relative to the peer set. The stage-progression method produces a floor valuation but may underweight market-rate pricing for Series A defense companies. Peer valuations themselves exhibit substantial dispersion, suggesting that differentiation in technology, customer concentration, or growth trajectory drives significant valuation variance in this sector.
